vendor.create.description_detailed: Creates a new vendor record in QuickBooks Online. Vendors are companies or individuals you pay (suppliers, contractors, service providers). The vendor_id returned can be used in bill.create to record money owed. Vendor names must be unique within QuickBooks - use vendor.search first to check for duplicates.

vendor_name.description: Display name for the vendor (must be unique in QuickBooks)

billing_address.description: Billing address with Line1, City, State, PostalCode

display_name.description: Confirmed display name as stored in QuickBooks

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when vendor was created

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow at /oauth/quickbooks/authorize

errors.description: Duplicate vendor name or missing required field
errors.recovery_hint: Use vendor.search first to check if vendor already exists

examples.description: Create a basic vendor with name and email

vendor.get.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific vendor by ID. Use this to get current contact information, status, and other vendor details. The vendor_id should include the 'qb:' prefix (e.g., 'qb:123').

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Vendor not found with given ID
errors.recovery_hint: Verify vendor_id is correct using vendor.search or vendor.list

vendor.list.description_detailed: Returns a paginated list of vendors from QuickBooks. By default returns only active vendors. Use this for bulk retrieval or when you need to see all vendors. For finding a specific vendor by name, use vendor.search instead.

max_results.description: Maximum number of vendors to return (1-1000)

active_only.description: If true, only return active vendors

count.description: Number of vendors returned in this response

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

vendor.search.description: Search vendors by name with fuzzy matching
vendor.search.description_detailed: Searches for vendors by name using fuzzy (LIKE) matching. Use this before vendor.create to check if a vendor already exists and avoid duplicates. Also useful for finding vendors when you only have a partial name.

search_term.description: Search term to match against vendor display names

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

count.description: Number of vendors matching the search

search_term.description: The search term that was used

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

examples.description: Search for vendors containing 'Office' in their name

vendor.update.description_detailed: Updates an existing vendor's information in QuickBooks. Only the fields provided will be updated - omitted fields retain their current values. QuickBooks requires the current SyncToken which is fetched automatically.

vendor_name.description: New display name (must be unique if changed)

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Duplicate vendor name or invalid field value
errors.recovery_hint: Check that new vendor_name is unique

errors.description: Vendor not found or stale SyncToken
errors.recovery_hint: Retry the update - SyncToken will be refreshed

bill.create.description_detailed: Creates a new bill (accounts payable) in QuickBooks. A bill represents money owed to a vendor for goods or services received. You must have a valid vendor_id (use vendor.search or vendor.create first). Line items specify what was purchased and can be linked to expense accounts or items.

line_items.description: Array of line items with Amount and detail (AccountRef or ItemRef)

due_date.description: Payment due date in YYYY-MM-DD format

txn_date.description: Transaction date in YYYY-MM-DD format (defaults to today)

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Invalid vendor_id, missing line items, or invalid account reference
errors.recovery_hint: Verify vendor exists with vendor.get, and account references are valid using chartofaccounts.get

examples.description: Create a bill for office supplies

bill.get.description: Get bill details including line items
bill.get.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific bill including its line items, current balance, and payment status. Use this to check if a bill has been partially or fully paid.

balance.description: Remaining unpaid balance (0 if fully paid)

vendor_id.description: Vendor ID this bill is from

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Bill not found with given ID
errors.recovery_hint: Verify bill_id is correct using bill.list

bill.list.description: List bills with optional vendor/date filters
bill.list.description_detailed: Returns a list of bills from QuickBooks with optional filtering. Use this to find bills for a specific vendor or to get a list of unpaid bills. Results are paginated.

vendor_id.description: Filter bills by vendor ID (with 'qb:' prefix)

unpaid_only.description: If true, only return bills with balance > 0

limit.description: Maximum number of bills to return

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

examples.description: List all unpaid bills for a vendor

bill.payment.create.description: Create a bill payment in QuickBooks
bill.payment.create.description_detailed: Records a payment made to a vendor for one or more bills. This creates a BillPayment transaction in QuickBooks and reduces the balance on the linked bills. You can pay multiple bills at once by providing multiple bill_ids.

vendor_id.description: QuickBooks vendor ID receiving the payment (with 'qb:' prefix)

bill_ids.description: Array of bill IDs to apply payment to (with 'qb:' prefix)

check_num.description: Check number if payment_type is Check

txn_date.description: Payment date in YYYY-MM-DD format (defaults to today)

payment_id.description: QuickBooks bill payment ID with 'qb:' prefix

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Invalid vendor_id, bill_ids, or payment amount exceeds bill balance
errors.recovery_hint: Verify vendor and bills exist, and payment amount is valid

examples.description: Pay a bill with a check

billpayment.list.description: List vendor bill payments with optional filters
billpayment.list.description_detailed: Returns a list of bill payments (money paid to vendors) from QuickBooks. Can be filtered by vendor to see payment history for a specific supplier.

vendor_id.description: Filter payments by vendor ID (with 'qb:' prefix)

limit.description: Maximum number of payments to return

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

examples.description: List recent payments to a vendor

qb.customer.create.description_detailed: Creates a new customer record in QuickBooks Online. Customers are individuals or businesses who purchase goods or services from you. The customer_id returned can be used in invoice.create and payment.create. Customer names should be unique.

customer_name.description: Display name for the customer (should be unique)

company_name.description: Company/business name (if different from display name)

billing_address.description: Billing address with Line1, City, CountrySubDivisionCode, PostalCode

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when customer was created

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Duplicate customer name or invalid field
errors.recovery_hint: Use qb.customer.search to check if customer exists

examples.description: Create a new business customer

qb.customer.get.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific customer including contact info, current balance, and status. Use this to verify customer details before creating invoices or to check outstanding balances.

qb.customer.update.description_detailed: Updates an existing customer's information. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields retain current values. Automatically handles SyncToken.

qb.customer.list.description_detailed: Returns a paginated list of customers. Use for bulk retrieval or browsing. For finding a specific customer by name, use qb.customer.search instead.

qb.customer.search.description: Search customers by name with fuzzy matching
qb.customer.search.description_detailed: Searches for customers using LIKE matching on display name. Use before qb.customer.create to check for duplicates or to find customers by partial name.

search_term.description: Search term to match against customer names

customer.create.description: Create a customer in QuickBooks (MARS alias)
customer.create.description_detailed: Creates a new customer record in QuickBooks Online. Customers are individuals or businesses who purchase goods or services from you. The customer_id returned can be used in invoice.create and payment.create. Customer names should be unique.

customer_name.description: Display name for the customer (should be unique)

company_name.description: Company/business name (if different from display name)

billing_address.description: Billing address with Line1, City, CountrySubDivisionCode, PostalCode

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when customer was created

errors.description: QuickBooks not connected for this user/org
errors.recovery_hint: User must complete QuickBooks OAuth flow

errors.description: Duplicate customer name or invalid field
errors.recovery_hint: Use qb.customer.search to check if customer exists

customer.get.description: Get customer details from QuickBooks (MARS alias)
customer.get.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific customer including contact info, current balance, and status. Use this to verify customer details before creating invoices or to check outstanding balances.

customer.update.description: Update a customer in QuickBooks (MARS alias)
customer.update.description_detailed: Updates an existing customer's information. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields retain current values. Automatically handles SyncToken.

customer.list.description_detailed: Returns a paginated list of customers. Use for bulk retrieval or browsing. For finding a specific customer by name, use qb.customer.search instead.

qb.invoice.create.description_detailed: Creates a new invoice (accounts receivable) for a customer. An invoice represents money owed to you for goods or services. Requires a valid customer_id and line items. The invoice can be sent via email using invoice.send after creation.

line_items.description: Array of line items with Amount, Description, and detail type

customer_memo.description: Message displayed on invoice to customer

examples.description: Create an invoice for consulting services

qb.invoice.get.description_detailed: Retrieves complete invoice details including line items, balance, and email status. Use to check payment status or get invoice details before sending.

qb.invoice.send.description: Send invoice via email through QuickBooks
qb.invoice.send.description_detailed: Sends an invoice to the customer via email using QuickBooks' email service. The email will be sent from your QuickBooks account and include a link for online payment.

email.description: Override email address (uses customer's email if not provided)

errors.recovery_hint: Provide email parameter or update customer email

qb.invoice.void.description_detailed: Voids an existing invoice, setting its balance to zero. Use this instead of deleting to maintain audit trail. Cannot void invoices with payments applied.

void_reason.description: Reason for voiding (stored in private note)

qb.invoice.list.description_detailed: Returns a list of invoices with optional filtering by customer or payment status. For outstanding invoices specifically, use qb.invoice.list_outstanding.

qb.invoice.list_outstanding.description: List outstanding invoices (Balance > 0) - CRITICAL for payment allocation
qb.invoice.list_outstanding.description_detailed: Returns only invoices with outstanding balances. Essential for MARS payment allocation workflows - identifies which invoices need payment.

invoice.create.description: Create an invoice in QuickBooks (MARS alias)
invoice.create.description_detailed: Creates a new invoice (accounts receivable) for a customer. An invoice represents money owed to you for goods or services. Requires a valid customer_id and line items. The invoice can be sent via email using invoice.send after creation.

line_items.description: Array of line items with Amount, Description, and detail type

customer_memo.description: Message displayed on invoice to customer

invoice.get.description_detailed: Retrieves complete invoice details including line items, balance, and email status. Use to check payment status or get invoice details before sending.

invoice.send.description_detailed: Sends an invoice to the customer via email using QuickBooks' email service. The email will be sent from your QuickBooks account and include a link for online payment.

email.description: Override email address (uses customer's email if not provided)

errors.recovery_hint: Provide email parameter or update customer email

invoice.void.description_detailed: Voids an existing invoice, setting its balance to zero. Use this instead of deleting to maintain audit trail. Cannot void invoices with payments applied.

void_reason.description: Reason for voiding (stored in private note)

invoice.list.description_detailed: Returns a list of invoices with optional filtering by customer or payment status. For outstanding invoices specifically, use qb.invoice.list_outstanding.

item.create.description_detailed: Creates a new item (product or service) that can be used on invoices and sales. Items can be Services, Inventory, or NonInventory types.

errors.recovery_hint: Use item.list to check existing items

qb.payment.create.description: Create a payment (customer payment) in QuickBooks
qb.payment.create.description_detailed: Records a payment received from a customer. Can be applied to specific invoices using invoice_ids, or recorded as unapplied payment.

invoice_ids.description: Invoice IDs to apply payment to

payment.apply_to_invoice.description: Apply payment to specific invoice(s) with multi-invoice allocation
payment.apply_to_invoice.description_detailed: Creates a payment and applies it to one or more invoices with specific amounts. Enables complex payment allocation scenarios where a single payment covers multiple invoices.

invoice_allocations.description: Array of {invoice_id, amount} for each invoice

payment.list.description: List customer payments with optional filters

estimate.create.description_detailed: Creates a quote/estimate for a customer. Estimates can be converted to invoices and are useful for quoting before work begins.

salesreceipt.create.description: Create a sales receipt in QuickBooks
salesreceipt.create.description_detailed: Creates a sales receipt for immediate payment transactions. Unlike invoices, sales receipts are used when payment is received at time of sale.

creditmemo.create.description: Create a credit memo in QuickBooks
creditmemo.create.description_detailed: Creates a credit memo to reduce a customer's balance. Can be applied to outstanding invoices or remain as available credit.

timeactivity.create.description: Create a time activity in QuickBooks
timeactivity.create.description_detailed: Records time worked by an employee or vendor. Can be marked billable and linked to a customer for invoicing.

refundreceipt.create.description_detailed: Creates a refund receipt to record money returned to a customer. Typically used after a sales receipt for returns.

journal.create.description: Create a journal entry in QuickBooks
journal.create.description_detailed: Creates a manual journal entry with debit and credit lines. Journal entries must balance (total debits = total credits). Use for adjustments, corrections, or transactions not captured by other transaction types.

lines.description: Journal lines with PostingType, Amount, and AccountRef

errors.description: Journal entry doesn't balance or invalid account
errors.recovery_hint: Ensure debits = credits and verify account IDs

examples.description: Create adjusting entry for accrued expenses

qb.query.description: Query QuickBooks entities using SQL-like syntax
qb.query.description_detailed: Execute SQL-like queries against QuickBooks entities. Supports SELECT with WHERE, ORDERBY, STARTPOSITION, and MAXRESULTS. Useful for complex queries not covered by other endpoints.

query.example: SELECT * FROM Invoice WHERE Balance > 0 MAXRESULTS 50

chartofaccounts.get.description: Get chart of accounts from QuickBooks
chartofaccounts.get.description_detailed: Retrieves the full chart of accounts with optional filtering by account type. Use to get account IDs needed for journal entries, bills, and other transactions.

account.list.description_detailed: Alias for chartofaccounts.get - retrieves all accounts.

account.get.description: Get single account details with balance
account.get.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific account including current balance.

purchaseorder.create.description: Create a purchase order in QuickBooks
purchaseorder.create.description_detailed: Creates a purchase order for a vendor. Purchase orders are used to order goods before receiving them and can be converted to bills upon receipt.

expense.create.description_detailed: Creates a purchase/expense transaction. Unlike bills, expenses are paid immediately (cash, credit card, etc.) rather than on account.

deposit.create.description_detailed: Creates a deposit to record money received into a bank account. Can include multiple line items from different sources.

deposit_account_id.description: Bank account ID to deposit into

line_items.description: Deposit line items with amounts and sources

qb.transfer.create.description_detailed: Creates a transfer between two accounts (typically bank accounts). Records money moving from one account to another.

errors.description: Invalid account IDs or insufficient funds

transaction.list.description_detailed: Returns a transaction list report showing all transactions within a date range. Useful for reconciliation and audit purposes.

report.profitloss.description_detailed: Generates a Profit & Loss (Income Statement) report showing revenue, expenses, and net income for a specified period. Can be filtered by class or location.

report.description: Full P&L report with rows, columns, and summary

report.balancesheet.description: Get Balance Sheet report from QuickBooks
report.balancesheet.description_detailed: Generates a Balance Sheet showing assets, liabilities, and equity as of a specific date. Provides a snapshot of financial position.

as_of_date.description: Report as-of date (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to today)

report.description: Balance sheet with assets, liabilities, equity sections

report.profitloss.detail.description: Get detailed P&L report with individual transactions
report.profitloss.detail.description_detailed: Generates a detailed Profit & Loss report showing individual transactions that make up each account balance. More granular than standard P&L.

report.cashflow.description: Get Cash Flow Statement report from QuickBooks
report.cashflow.description_detailed: Generates a Cash Flow Statement showing cash from operating, investing, and financing activities. Shows how cash position changed over period.

report.description: Cash flow statement with operating/investing/financing sections

report.generalledger.description_detailed: Generates a General Ledger report showing all transactions by account. The most detailed transaction report available.

report.description: General ledger with all account transactions

report.ar_aging.description_detailed: Generates an Accounts Receivable Aging Summary showing customer balances grouped by aging buckets (Current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days).

report.description: AR aging summary by customer and bucket

report.ap_aging.description_detailed: Generates an Accounts Payable Aging Summary showing vendor balances grouped by aging buckets. Helps prioritize vendor payments.

report.description: AP aging summary by vendor and bucket

report.ar_aging_detail.description: Get detailed AR Aging with individual invoices
report.ar_aging_detail.description_detailed: Generates a detailed Accounts Receivable Aging report showing each individual invoice and its age. More granular than summary.

report.description: Detailed AR aging with invoice-level detail

report.ap_aging_detail.description: Get detailed AP Aging with individual bills
report.ap_aging_detail.description_detailed: Generates a detailed Accounts Payable Aging report showing each individual bill and its age. Useful for payment prioritization.

report.description: Detailed AP aging with bill-level detail

budget.get.description: Get budget data for variance analysis (actual vs budget comparison)
budget.get.description_detailed: Retrieves budget data from QuickBooks for comparison against actual performance. Use for variance analysis and financial planning.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year to retrieve budget for

document.upload.description: Upload document (W-9, invoice, receipt) and attach to entity
document.upload.description_detailed: Uploads a document file and attaches it to a QuickBooks entity like a vendor, customer, bill, or invoice. Useful for storing W-9s, receipts, and source documents.

file_path.description: Local path to the file to upload

entity_type.description: Type of entity to attach to

errors.description: File not found or unsupported format
errors.recovery_hint: Verify file path and format (PDF, PNG, JPG supported)

class.list.description_detailed: Retrieves all classes defined in QuickBooks. Classes are used for categorization and tracking (e.g., by project, product line, or business segment).

department.list.description_detailed: Retrieves all departments (locations) defined in QuickBooks. Departments are used for location tracking and reporting.

employee.list.description_detailed: Retrieves all employees defined in QuickBooks. Employees can be assigned to time activities and used for tracking billable work.

employee.get.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific employee.

term.list.description_detailed: Retrieves all payment terms defined in QuickBooks (e.g., Net 30, Due on Receipt). Terms define when payment is due and any early payment discounts.

taxcode.list.description_detailed: Retrieves all tax codes defined in QuickBooks. Tax codes determine how sales tax is calculated on transactions.

company.info.description_detailed: Retrieves company profile information including name, address, contact details, and fiscal year settings.

netsuite.journal.create.description: Create a journal entry in NetSuite
netsuite.journal.create.description_detailed: Creates a journal entry in NetSuite's general ledger. CRITICAL: Total debits must equal total credits or the entry will be rejected. Each line requires an account ID and either a debit or credit amount. Uses REST API with TBA or OAuth 2.0 authentication.

subsidiary_id.description: Internal ID of the subsidiary (OneWorld only)

lines.description: Journal entry lines (each with account_id and debit OR credit)

memo.description: Header memo for the journal entry

errors.description: Debits and credits do not balance
errors.recovery_hint: Ensure total debits equal total credits before submitting
User must complete TBA or OAuth 2.0 authentication

errors.description: INVALID_KEY_OR_REF - Invalid account or subsidiary ID
errors.recovery_hint: Verify account IDs exist using netsuite.account.list

netsuite.journal.get.description: Get a journal entry by ID
netsuite.journal.get.description_detailed: Retrieves a journal entry from NetSuite by its internal ID. Returns full details including all line items with account information, debits, credits, and any segment values (department, class, location).

journal_entry_id.description: NetSuite internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

lines.description: Line items with account, debit/credit amounts

netsuite.vendorbill.create.description: Create a vendor bill in NetSuite
netsuite.vendorbill.create.description_detailed: Creates a vendor bill (accounts payable) in NetSuite. Vendor bills track money owed to suppliers. Each bill has expense lines with account allocations. Bills can be approved and paid via vendor payments.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

expense_lines.description: Expense line items with account_id and amount

errors.description: Invalid vendor_id or missing required fields
INVALID_KEY_OR_REF - Invalid account ID in expense lines
errors.recovery_hint: Verify account IDs exist using netsuite.account.list

netsuite.vendorbill.get.description: Get a vendor bill by ID
netsuite.vendorbill.get.description_detailed: Retrieves a vendor bill from NetSuite by its internal ID. Returns full details including expense lines, payment status, and approval status.

bill_id.description: Vendor bill internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

netsuite.vendorbill.list.description_detailed: Lists vendor bills using SuiteQL query. Supports filtering by vendor, date range, and status. Returns up to 1000 results per page.

netsuite.vendorbill.approve.description_detailed: Sets the approval status of a vendor bill to Approved. Only applicable if bill approvals are enabled in NetSuite. Approved bills can then be scheduled for payment.

bill_id.description: Vendor bill internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

errors.description: Bill not found or already approved
errors.recovery_hint: Verify bill exists and is pending approval

netsuite.vendor.get.description_detailed: Retrieves a vendor record from NetSuite by its internal ID. Returns company information, contact details, and current balance.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

errors.recovery_hint: Verify the vendor_id is correct using netsuite.vendor.search

netsuite.vendor.create.description_detailed: Creates a new vendor record in NetSuite. Vendors are entities you pay - suppliers, contractors, service providers. The vendor_id returned can be used to create bills and payments.

account_number.description: Your account number with this vendor

errors.description: Duplicate vendor name or missing required field
errors.recovery_hint: Use netsuite.vendor.search first to check for duplicates

netsuite.vendor.update.description_detailed: Updates an existing vendor record in NetSuite. Can update contact info, address, payment terms, and other fields. Only provided fields are updated - others remain unchanged.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

address.description: Address object with street1, street2, city, state, zip, country

account_number.description: Your account number with this vendor

errors.description: Vendor not found or duplicate name

netsuite.vendor.search.description: Search for vendors by company name
netsuite.vendor.search.description_detailed: Searches for vendors in NetSuite by company name using SuiteQL. Returns matching vendors with basic info. Use before creating vendors to avoid duplicates.

vendor_name.description: Company name to search for (partial match supported)

errors.recovery_hint: Provide a vendor_name to search for

netsuite.vendor.upload_document.description_detailed: Uploads a document (W-9, contract, etc.) to NetSuite File Cabinet and attaches it to a vendor record. File content must be base64 encoded.

vendor_id.description: Vendor the file is attached to

errors.recovery_hint: Ensure vendor exists and file_content is valid base64

netsuite.payment.create.description_detailed: Creates a vendor payment in NetSuite to pay one or more bills. Specify the vendor, bank account, and bills to pay. Payments can be partial or full payment of bills.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

bank_account_id.description: Bank account internal ID to pay from

bills_to_pay.description: Bills to pay with optional amounts

payment_method.description: Payment method internal ID (check, ACH, wire, etc.)

errors.description: Invalid vendor, bank account, or bill IDs
errors.recovery_hint: Verify all IDs exist before creating payment

errors.description: Bill already paid or insufficient funds

netsuite.payment.batch.description_detailed: Processes multiple vendor payments in a batch. Each payment is processed independently - failures don't affect others. Returns results and errors for each payment.

payments.description: Array of payment specifications (same format as payment.create)

errors.recovery_hint: Ensure each payment has required fields

netsuite.purchaseorder.create.description_detailed: Creates a purchase order in NetSuite. POs are used to order items from vendors. When received, POs can be converted to vendor bills.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

item_lines.description: Item lines with item_id, quantity, and optional rate

netsuite.account.list.description_detailed: Retrieves the chart of accounts from NetSuite using SuiteQL. Returns account numbers, names, types, and balances. Use to look up account IDs for journal entries and bills.

account_type.description: Filter by account type (Bank, Expense, Income, etc.)

accounts.description: List of accounts with id, number, name, type, balance

netsuite.subsidiary.list.description_detailed: Retrieves the list of subsidiaries from a NetSuite OneWorld account. Subsidiary IDs are required for journal entries and transactions. Non-OneWorld accounts have a single implicit subsidiary.

subsidiaries.description: List of subsidiaries with id, name, country

netsuite.gl.transactions.description_detailed: Retrieves general ledger transaction lines from NetSuite using SuiteQL. Essential for bank reconciliation workflows - matches bank statement lines to GL entries. Filter by account and date range.

account_ids.description: Account internal IDs to filter (omit for all accounts)

transactions.description: GL transaction lines with account, debit/credit, memo

errors.description: Invalid date range or account IDs
errors.recovery_hint: Use YYYY-MM-DD format and verify account IDs

netsuite.reconcile.bank.description_detailed: Compares bank statement balance against NetSuite GL balance for a specific account and date. Returns variance information. Note: Actual reconciliation is typically done in NetSuite UI.

netsuite.query.description_detailed: Executes a SuiteQL query against NetSuite. SuiteQL is NetSuite's SQL-like query language. Max 100,000 total results, 1,000 per page. Use for custom data retrieval not covered by other capabilities.

query.example: SELECT id, companyname FROM vendor WHERE isinactive = 'F'

errors.recovery_hint: Check query syntax and table/column names
Verify table and column names exist in NetSuite

netsuite.customrecord.create.description_detailed: Creates a custom record in NetSuite. Custom records are user-defined record types created in NetSuite. Record type ID format: customrecord_XXX

fields.description: Field values for the custom record

errors.recovery_hint: Verify record type exists and fields are valid

sage_intacct.company.get.description_detailed: Retrieves company details including name, ID, and configuration.

errors.recovery_hint: User must connect Sage Intacct account

sage_intacct.accounts.list.description: List chart of accounts from Sage Intacct
sage_intacct.accounts.list.description_detailed: Lists GL accounts with optional filtering by type and status.

sage_intacct.accounts.create.description: Create a new GL account in Sage Intacct

sage_intacct.journals.list.description: List journal entries from Sage Intacct

sage_intacct.journals.create.description: Create a journal entry in Sage Intacct
sage_intacct.journals.create.description_detailed: Creates a journal entry with debit and credit lines. Total debits must equal total credits.

lines.description: Journal lines with account_no, amount, memo, debit/credit

sage_intacct.journals.post.description: Post a draft journal entry in Sage Intacct

sage_intacct.vendors.create.description: Create a vendor in Sage Intacct

sage_intacct.bills.list.description: List AP bills from Sage Intacct

sage_intacct.bills.create.description: Create an AP bill in Sage Intacct

lines.description: Bill lines with gl_account, amount, memo

sage_intacct.bills.post.description: Post a draft AP bill in Sage Intacct

sage_intacct.bill_payments.create.description: Create a bill payment in Sage Intacct

bills.description: Bills to pay with bill_key and amount

sage_intacct.customers.create.description: Create a customer in Sage Intacct

stripe.payment.create.description: Create a payment intent in Stripe
